question.text: Under the new tax regime for FY 2024-25, what is the income tax on a total income of Rs. 14,00,000 before surcharge and cess?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** New regime FY 2024-25 slabs: 0-3L = Nil; 3-7L = 5%; 7-10L = 10%; 10-12L = 15%; 12-15L = 20%; >15L = 30%. On total income of Rs. 14,00,000: Up to 3L = Nil; 3-7L = 4L × 5% = Rs. 20,000; 7-10L = 3L × 10% = Rs. 30,000; 10-12L = 2L × 15% = Rs. 30,000; 12-14L = 2L × 20% = Rs. 40,000. Total = Rs. 20,000 + 30,000 + 30,000 + 40,000 = Rs. 1,20,000. **The Trap:** Rs. 1,50,000 results from applying 30% instead of 20% on the 12-14L bracket. Rs. 1,30,000 comes from using 15% instead of 10% on the 7-10L bracket. Rs. 1,70,000 results from applying FY 2025-26 slab structure to FY 2024-25 income. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-B tests awareness of slab differences across financial years — a common trap in exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Tax slabs under the new regime changed between FY 2024-25 and FY 2025-26 (slabs became wider with higher thresholds). Always use the correct year's slabs when projecting client tax liability.
